stories.title: Halloween in the Palace of Splendor
stories.excerpt: Five weeks after Nevermind, the most important band in the world played their biggest show yet — for ten dollars a ticket, in their own city, in a room built for silent films.

stories.title: A Brief Lightning Bolt
stories.excerpt: Their final show. A band formed in a rehab clinic in Minnesota returned to Seattle and made one extraordinary album before the darkness closed in.

stories.title: The Godfather Comes to Capitol Hill
stories.excerpt: He showed up with one guitar, no roadie, and docked his boat in the harbour each night to write new songs. Four days later, Mirror Ball existed.

stories.title: Before the Box
stories.excerpt: Facelift hasn't been recorded yet. Seattle's oldest bar — a Gold Rush saloon now witnessing the birth of something new.

stories.title: The Night Before Everything Changed
stories.excerpt: Three days before Nevermind dropped, Nirvana played a basement bar in Montréal. Nobody in the room knew what was about to happen.

stories.title: Without Jerry
stories.excerpt: Less than a year after Jerry Garcia died, the Grateful Dead family tried to keep the music alive. An outdoor ballpark in Maine. A crowd that needed to believe.

stories.title: The Girl Who Came Home
stories.excerpt: Seven days after Jagged Little Pill was released, Alanis Morissette came back to Ottawa and played the bar where she got her start.

stories.title: Two Worlds Collide on Halloween
stories.excerpt: Halloween night in Chicago. Bob Dylan played the first set. Phil Lesh and Friends played the second. Two separate gravitational fields sharing one arena.

stories.title: Four Minutes and Fifteen Seconds
stories.excerpt: Seventeen songs in. The Troubadour in West Hollywood. And then, finally, The Rat — the song that sounds like a building on fire.

stories.title: We Can Be Heroes
stories.excerpt: The song was recorded 500 yards from the Berlin Wall. A year later, Bowie played it at Earls Court. The wall was still standing. The crowd sang every word anyway.

stories.title: Dressed to Thrill
stories.excerpt: Seven years of silence, then a Camden basement. The band that Pulp Fiction made famous came back to prove the fire was still there.

stories.title: The Longest Song in the World
stories.excerpt: A half-empty exhibition hall. The Fear of the Dark tour. A song played in its first week of existence that would become one of the great anthems in rock.

stories.title: The Mystic and the King
stories.excerpt: The mystic who fights for transcendence and the king who arrives already carrying it. The same audience. The same night. The room next to Congo Square.

stories.title: New Year's Eve at the Home of the Blues
stories.excerpt: Stevie Ray Vaughan had been dead for four months and four days. His rhythm section, and two guitarists who had learned from him, walked into his favourite room and played.

stories.title: Raw Power at the House of Piaf
stories.excerpt: Three weeks after his only US top 40 hit, Iggy Pop walked onto the stage at L'Olympia — Piaf's room, the Beatles' room — and opened with Raw Power. The culture had finally turned back toward him.
